message.content:
Based on the provided screenshot of the resume builder page, here's a detailed breakdown of your questions:  

### 1) Is the resume preview visible in the center?  
No. The **center area (where the resume preview would typically appear)** is not visible in the screenshot. The image only shows the left sidebar and a partial view of the right panel.    

### 2) Is the right panel/editing panel visible?  
Partially. A small section of the right panel is visible (dark background), but it does not display any editable content or preview. It appears to be a narrow, empty strip.    

### 3) Are there any buttons that look non-functional or greyed out?  
No. The visible buttons (e.g., “Editor,” “Templates,” “Examples,” etc.) in the left sidebar are not greyed out; they have standard styling (white text on dark background, with some highlighted in purple).    

### 4) Can you see the full layout - left sidebar, center preview, right panel?  
No. The **center preview area is missing**. Only the left sidebar (with menu items) and a partial right panel are visible. The full three-panel layout (left sidebar + center preview + right editing panel) is not displayed.    

### 5) Is the page missing any major sections?  
Yes. The **center resume preview section** (the core component of a resume builder) is missing. This is a major omission, as the preview is essential for users to see their resume in real time.    

### 6) Are there any visual glitches or broken layouts?  
No obvious visual glitches. However, the layout is incomplete (missing the center preview), which could be a design flaw or a loading issue. The left sidebar and partial right panel appear structurally intact.    

### 7) What exactly is the user seeing - is it just the sidebar or the full page?  
The user is seeing **only the left sidebar** (with menu options like “Editor,” “Templates,” etc.) and a **partial, empty right panel**. The center preview (the main resume display area) is not visible, so the “full page” layout is incomplete.    


In summary, the screenshot shows an incomplete view of the resume builder, with the left sidebar fully visible but the critical center preview and full right panel missing.
